description: Model-defined common household-consumption price index used as the numeraire.
JCGECore numeraire kind for a model-defined price index.
The sole global investment pool whose zero-sum transfer identity is evaluated after solution as an accounting check.
Region of the composite-market identity evaluated after solution to remove the Walras-redundant market condition.
Industry code of the composite-market identity evaluated after solution to remove the Walras-redundant market condition.
Strictly positive lower bound supplied to JCGE blocks for prices, outputs, and bilateral trade flows.
Fraction of the smallest positive calibrated starting value used as Ipopt's absolute interior-point bound push.
Minimum equation scale in the calibrated monetary unit. It prevents rounding differences in very small accounts from dominating the scaled baseline system.
Ipopt Hessian treatment for the large nonlinear feasibility system.
Ipopt bound-multiplier initialization consistent with a feasible calibrated start point.
Initial Ipopt barrier parameter for the calibrated feasibility solve.
Ipopt convergence tolerance used for calibration replication.
Ipopt acceptable convergence tolerance used for calibration replication.

Absolute residual threshold for reporting the baseline replication. The physical METAL quantities are expressed in tonnes, so this corresponds to 0.1 kg and is far below the resolution of the source accounts.
Common calibration price used to express the monetary SAM as model quantities at the reference point.
Initial Armington elasticity for observed product sourcing; retained as a sensitivity parameter until product-specific evidence is selected.
Initial CET transformation elasticity for observed product sales; retained as a sensitivity parameter until product-specific evidence is selected.
Reference delivery-price wedge for observed trade routes; transport margins are represented in the monetary calibration accounts.
Reference world price for the price-taking rest-of-world endpoint.
Final-demand codes aggregated into regional household consumption.
Final-demand code for regional government consumption.
Final-demand code for gross fixed capital formation.
Final-demand code for changes in inventories, retained from gross output before trade allocation and financed through saving-investment rather than treated as goods-market demand or fixed investment.
